title: Anne van Biema Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Japanese Visual Arts at the Smithsonian
summary: The Anne van Biema Fellowship 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for advanced scholars to conduct in-depth research on Japanese visual arts at one of the world’s most prestigious institutions—the Smithsonian’s National Museum of Asian Art in Washington, DC. This highly competitive fellowship is designed to support innovative and impactful research that contributes to both academic scholarship and public understanding of Japanese art. With generous funding, access to world-class collections, and an intellectually stimulating environment, this program is ideal for researchers seeking to elevate their work to an international level. About the Anne van Biema Fellowship The Anne van Biema Fellowship was established through a bequest with the goal of promoting excellence in research and publication in Japanese visual arts . It supports scholars working on projects that demonstrate: Strong academic merit Originality and innovation Clear and rigorous methodology Potential for significant scholarly publication The fellowship welcomes interdisciplinary research , provided that the primary focus remains on Japanese visual arts. Host Institution: Smithsonian’s National Museum of Asian Art Fellows will conduct their research at the Smithsonian’s National Museum of Asian Art, which houses one of the most important collections of Asian art in the world. Participants benefit from: Access to extensive art collections and archives Use of Smithsonian libraries and research facilities A collaborative academic environment with leading scholars This setting provides an ideal platform for producing high-quality research with global impact. Fellowship Duration and Structure For the 2026 cycle, the fellowship duration is: 3 to 9 months Appointments begin between August 1 and December 1, 2026 Fellows are not required to reside in Washington, DC for the entire duration, offering flexibility while maintaining access to institutional resources. Funding and Benefits The Anne van Biema Fellowship provides comprehensive financial and institutional support: Stipend Up to $45,000 for 3–9 months Approximately $5,000 per month , prorated based on duration Additional Research Support Up to $5,000 for research and travel expenses Institutional Resources Dedicated research space Access to a networked computer Full access to collections, archives, and libraries Academic Expectations Fellows are expected to: Dedicate full-time effort to their research project Actively participate in the museum’s scholarly community Engage in academic discussions and programs Present a research-in-progress seminar during their fellowship This ensures both intellectual exchange and professional visibility within the academic community. Eligibility Criteria The fellowship is open to: Postdoctoral and senior scholars Researchers with a strong background in: Japanese visual arts Art history Related interdisciplinary fields Nationality Open to all nationalities worldwide Visa Information Applicants are responsible for meeting U.S. visa requirements. If necessary, the Smithsonian may support eligible candidates through the J-1 Visa Exchange Visitor Program . Application Requirements Applicants must submit a complete application package in English, including: Application cover sheet Curriculum Vitae (CV) with full publication list Research proposal: 250-word précis Maximum 2,000-word narrative Budget plan (maximum $5,000 for research expenses) Two letters of recommendation All application materials must be submitted via email. Application Deadline and Timeline Application Deadline: May 4, 2026 Notification of Results: By the end of June 2026 Fellowship Start Period: Between August 1 and December 1, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure all materials, including recommendation letters, are submitted on time. Why You Should Apply The Anne van Biema Fellowship is more than just funding—it is a gateway to academic excellence and international recognition. It offers scholars the chance to: Conduct research in a world-renowned institution Access rare and valuable art collections and archives Produce impactful publications in Japanese art history Collaborate with leading experts in the field Strengthen their academic and professional reputation For scholars dedicated to advancing the study of Japanese visual arts, this fellowship represents a prestigious and transformative opportunity. For more information click here Discover more information here The post Anne van Biema Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Japanese Visual Arts at the Smithsonian appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: African Food Research Acceleration Program (AFRAP) 2026: Fully-Funded Virtual Fellowship for Emerging African Food Scientists
summary: The African Food Research Acceleration Program (AFRAP) Cohort II is now open for applications, offering a unique opportunity for early-career food scientists across Africa to gain hands-on research experience, mentorship, and publication support — all at no cost. Designed by the Centre for African Food Research and Innovation (CAFRI) , AFRAP is a six-month, fully virtual fellowship that equips participants with the practical skills needed to design, execute, and publish original research focused on Africa’s food systems. What is AFRAP? The African Food Research Acceleration Program (AFRAP) is a structured, mentorship-led fellowship aimed at building the next generation of African food researchers. It focuses on empowering participants with real-world research skills while addressing critical challenges in African food systems — from production to consumption. Participants work closely with experienced mentors, attend expert-led workshops, and develop a publishable research manuscript as their main output. Programme Highlights Duration: 6 months Format: Fully virtual Cost: Free (no application fee) Mentorship: 1:1 mentor matching Outcome: One publishable research manuscript Network: Pan-African cohort of researchers What You Will Gain 1. Research Methodology Design Skills Learn how to formulate research questions, design studies, and develop strong research proposals. 2. Expert Mentorship Work closely with experienced researchers who guide you throughout the entire research process. 3. Data Analysis Expertise Gain hands-on experience with both quantitative and qualitative data analysis tools. 4. Publication-Ready Manuscript Graduate with a research paper ready for submission to peer-reviewed journals. 5. Pan-African Professional Network Collaborate with peers and build lasting relationships with researchers across the continent. Programme Structure The AFRAP fellowship is built around four key pillars: Research Design Develop strong research questions, objectives, and methodologies. Literature Mastery Conduct comprehensive literature reviews and identify research gaps. Data Analysis Learn data collection methods and analysis techniques. Manuscript Writing Structure and finalize a scientific paper for publication. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a citizen or resident of any African country Be a final-year undergraduate, master’s student, or early-career professional in a food-related field Demonstrate a strong interest in food science, nutrition, food safety, or food systems Be able to commit 4–6 hours per week for six months Have access to a computer and reliable internet connection Note: No prior research experience is required. Application Process To apply for AFRAP Cohort II, follow these steps: Review the eligibility criteria Prepare your CV or LinkedIn profile Complete the online application form Write three short motivation essays Submit your application Participate in an interview (if shortlisted) Receive onboarding materials upon acceptance APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Deadline Application Deadline: 30 June 2026 Early application is strongly encouraged as the selection process is competitive. Final Thoughts The AFRAP fellowship is more than just a training program — it is a launchpad for African food researchers to build credible research profiles, publish impactful work, and contribute to solving real challenges in Africa’s food systems. If you are passionate about food research and ready to develop your skills under expert guidance, this program offers a powerful opportunity to grow and make a meaningful impact. title: ifa Cultural Heritage Scholarship 2026 in Germany: Fully Funded Programme for Curators, Culture Managers, and Heritage Professionals
summary: The ifa (Institut für Auslandsbeziehungen) Cultural Heritage Scholarship 2026, supported by the Alexander Rave Foundation, is a prestigious fully funded international programme designed for cultural practitioners from developing countries to advance their expertise in cultural heritage management, museum transformation, and transcultural dialogue. This highly competitive scholarship offers participants the opportunity to undertake a 3 to 6-month professional stay in Germany, working closely with host institutions such as museums, galleries, and cultural research centers. The programme is rooted in fostering global collaboration, strengthening democratic values, and promoting intercultural understanding through heritage, archives, and collections. With a strong emphasis on sustainability, inclusion, and decolonial practices, this scholarship represents a transformative opportunity for professionals seeking to contribute to the evolution of cultural institutions worldwide. About the ifa Cultural Heritage Scholarship The ifa Cultural Heritage Scholarship is part of ifa’s broader mission to promote international cultural exchange, dialogue, and cooperation. Through this programme, cultural professionals engage in transnational learning, knowledge exchange, and collaborative project development. The scholarship is specifically designed to: Strengthen global networks in cultural heritage Encourage interdisciplinary collaboration Promote freedom of expression and academic discourse Foster democratic and inclusive cultural spaces Programme Overview Key Details Host Country: Germany Duration: 3 to 6 months Optional Reciprocal Stay: 2 to 12 weeks (Germany to DAC country) Application Deadline: June 30, 2026 Earliest Start Date: February 1, 2027 Programme End Date: December 31, 2027 Applicants will be notified of results by September 2026. Thematic Focus Areas The scholarship focuses on the transformation of museums and exhibition spaces in line with the International Council of Museums (ICOM) definition (2023). Core Themes Cultural participation Sustainability in cultural institutions Non-discriminatory and inclusive spaces Decolonial approaches to cultural heritage These themes reflect contemporary global discussions on the role of culture in shaping equitable and inclusive societies. Who Can Apply The programme is open to professionals working in cultural and heritage sectors. Eligible Applicants Include Curators Restorers Cultural mediators Culture managers Applicants must be actively engaged in work related to: Cultural heritage Collections Archive records Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the scholarship, applicants must meet the following requirements: Academic and Professional Requirements Completed higher education or vocational training Demonstrated professional experience in cultural institutions Strong involvement in heritage, collections, or archives Language Requirements Very good command of English Knowledge of German or the host country’s language is an advantage but not mandatory Additional Requirements Must secure a non-commercial host institution Must submit a jointly developed project proposal with the host institution Must not hold another scholarship during the funding period Host Institution Requirements Host institutions play a critical role in the success of the programme. Host Institutions Must: Be non-profit organizations Be museums, galleries, or cultural/research institutes Have a physical location Possess a collection or archive Assign a dedicated contact person Collaborate with the applicant on a project proposal Scholarship Benefits The ifa Cultural Heritage Scholarship offers comprehensive financial and logistical support. Financial Benefits Monthly stipend of EUR 1,500 Family allowance: EUR 250 for spouse EUR 250 per child Additional Support Travel costs (round trip) Visa costs Health insurance (including family coverage) Monthly public transport pass Up to EUR 500 for language courses Programme Structure Participants will: Work on a project related to collections or institutional transformation Engage in knowledge exchange with host institutions Contribute to sustainable cultural practices Participate in professional and academic discussions At the end of the programme, fellows must submit a final report documenting their work and learning outcomes. Application Process The application process is structured and requires careful preparation. Key Steps Identify a suitable host institution Develop a joint project proposal Prepare all required documents Submit the application via the official online form Click HERE to apply Important Notes Applications must be complete before submission Temporary saving of applications is not possible Applications submitted via email will not be accepted Late or incomplete applications will be rejected Selection and Award Details Maximum of 10 scholarships awarded annually Selection is highly competitive Projects must demonstrate strong relevance and impact Inclusivity and Diversity The programme actively encourages applications from: Women Black individuals and people of color LGBTQIA+ individuals Persons with disabilities Individuals from historically marginalized or colonized contexts This reflects the programme’s commitment to equity, diversity, and inclusive cultural development. Why You Should Apply The ifa Cultural Heritage Scholarship provides a unique opportunity to: Gain international experience in Germany Work with leading cultural institutions Expand professional networks Develop innovative approaches to cultural heritage Contribute to global conversations on inclusion and sustainability Learn more about the ifa (Institut für Auslandsbeziehungen) Cultural Heritage Scholarship 2026 HERE. Discover more global Scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE. title: Call for Grant Proposals: Strengthening CSO Capacities in the Maldives
summary: The Global Environment Facility Small Grants Programme (GEF SGP) in the Maldives has announced a Capacity Development Grant for Civil Society Organizations (CSOs) , community-based organizations (CBOs), and research institutions. With a grant of up to US$75,000 and applications open until 18 April 2026 , this initiative aims to strengthen the technical skills, proposal development, and monitoring and evaluation (M E) capacities of local organizations. By equipping Maldivian CSOs with the tools to design high-quality proposals and manage projects effectively, this grant promotes sustainable development, community wellbeing, and environmental stewardship. Objectives of the Grant The Capacity Development Grant is part of the eighth operational phase of the SGP (OP8), which builds on over 30 years of empowering local civil society. The grant focuses on three key areas: Grant Proposal Writing and Technical Skills Selected organizations will conduct workshops and training sessions to improve the ability of CSOs and CBOs to write compelling and fundable grant proposals. These sessions will include guidance on donor expectations, structuring proposals, common pitfalls, and practical exercises. One dedicated workshop will focus on helping existing SGP grantees with interim and final report submissions, strengthening their compliance with funding requirements. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Knowledge Management Organizations will support grantees in monitoring and documenting project outcomes, using simplified M E tools, ensuring data quality, and developing reporting frameworks. This component aims to enhance grantees’ ability to track progress, analyze results, and disseminate lessons learned effectively, allowing successful projects to be scaled and replicated. Advocacy and Communication The grant also includes conducting a two-year advocacy campaign to raise awareness of the work and successes of Maldivian CSOs. This involves producing communication materials such as social media content, infographics, short videos, and reports, as well as organizing webinars, virtual networks, and roundtable meetings with stakeholders. Through these activities, CSOs can amplify their impact, influence policy, and foster multi-stakeholder collaboration. Why This Opportunity Is Important Local civil society organizations are vital actors in addressing social, environmental, and economic challenges in the Maldives. However, many face limitations in accessing funding, managing projects, and effectively communicating their results. By enhancing capacity in proposal writing, M E, and advocacy, this grant equips CSOs with the skills necessary to secure resources, manage projects efficiently, and generate meaningful impact in their communities. Moreover, the grant encourages the sharing of knowledge and best practices, promoting innovation and replication of successful projects. By linking CSOs, CBOs, local councils, line ministries, and development partners, the initiative strengthens networks and fosters collaboration. This ultimately leads to more sustainable community-driven development initiatives, improved environmental resilience, and empowerment of marginalized groups, including women, youth, and persons with disabilities. Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to: National and local CSOs and NGOs Community-based organizations (CBOs) Research bodies with relevant experience Eligible organizations must demonstrate experience in capacity building, proposal development, M E support, and advocacy campaigns. The team should include members with social sciences or management backgrounds, expertise in communications, and hands-on experience with community development projects. How to Apply Interested organizations should submit: A detailed proposal outlining methodology and approach for all three components (workshops, M E support, advocacy). A budget and work plan covering a period of 24–36 months (2026–2028). CVs of proposed team members with clearly defined roles. A letter of application describing relevant experience and capacity. All documents must be submitted as attachments; externally hosted or editable links will not be accepted. Applications will be evaluated solely on materials received by the deadline. Apply by: 18 April 2026 | 23:00 HRS (MVT) For more information and submission details, visit the official call for proposals . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: ITC Tech and Services Internship 2026 – Gain Hands-On Experience in Global Trade and Innovation
summary: The International Trade Centre (ITC) is offering a fully immersive Tech and Services Internship in Geneva, Switzerland, providing emerging professionals with a unique opportunity to engage with international trade, innovation, and entrepreneurship. This internship is designed for students or recent graduates interested in supporting small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and startups in Africa, Asia, Latin America, and the Caribbean, while gaining practical experience in project management, research, and international development. About the Internship The ITC Tech and Services Internship runs for 4 to 6 months , starting 1 May 2026 . Interns will work under the guidance of the ITC’s Sector and Enterprise Competitiveness Section (SEC), particularly within the Tech and Services team. This team implements projects that support SMEs and startups, helping them grow, access new markets, and strengthen their operational ecosystems. Interns will contribute to ITC’s FastTrackTech methodology , assisting in the development of tools and resources aimed at fostering innovation in services sectors such as IT/BPO, logistics, tourism, and other high-potential markets. This role offers the opportunity to gain hands-on experience in program design, toolkit development, and collaboration with stakeholders across different regions. Why This Internship Matters SMEs and startups are key drivers of economic growth and job creation in emerging markets. By supporting these enterprises, the internship contributes to global development objectives, helping businesses scale sustainably, strengthen local economies, and generate employment opportunities. The internship also provides a window into international development operations within the UN system, allowing participants to understand the structure, goals, and processes of global trade and sector-specific programmes. For aspiring professionals in international business, development, or technology, this experience builds both professional competence and a global perspective on economic development. What Interns Will Gain The internship offers multiple professional and personal development benefits: Project Management Skills: Interns assist in implementing and testing ITC methodologies, organize pilot activities, and support reporting and monitoring frameworks. Research and Sector Analysis: Conduct market research, map priority service sectors, and prepare case studies and briefing materials for global projects. Global Partnerships: Engage with public and private stakeholders to strengthen innovation ecosystems and develop collaborative solutions for SMEs. Communication and Reporting Experience: Support the production of reports, toolkits, digital communications, and promotional materials to disseminate project outcomes. These experiences help interns build a strong foundation for careers in international trade, development, consulting, or technology-focused entrepreneurship. Who Can Apply This internship is open to candidates who meet one of the following criteria : Currently enrolled in a graduate program (second university degree or higher) Enrolled in the final year of a bachelor’s degree program Graduated with a university degree within the past year Candidates should have a strong interest in international trade, services sector development, technology, and innovation. Relevant studies may include business, international trade, development, or related fields. Applicants should also possess strong communication, analytical, and computer skills, including proficiency in Microsoft Office and social media. Fluency in English is required, and knowledge of French or Spanish is an advantage. Location and Duration The internship is based at ITC Headquarters in Geneva, Switzerland , and lasts 4 to 6 months , starting 1 May 2026. Interns are expected to manage their own living and travel arrangements, including visa and accommodation requirements. A subsistence allowance will be provided. Application Process and Deadline Applicants must submit their application through the official United Nations Careers portal. The deadline to apply is 13 April 2026 . Candidates should prepare a strong application highlighting relevant academic, research, or professional experiences, as well as their interest in supporting SMEs, technology, and services sector development. Learn More and Apply on the official ITC job posting . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: UNESCO MGIEP Youth Fellowship 2026 in South Korea: Fully Funded Leadership Programme for Indian Youth on Peace, Sustainability, and Global Impact
summary: The UNESCO MGIEP Youth Fellowship on Leadership for Peace and Sustainability 2026 is a prestigious, fully funded international fellowship designed to empower young Indian leaders with the knowledge, skills, and networks needed to promote peacebuilding, social innovation, and sustainable development. Hosted by the Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Education for Peace and Sustainable Development (MGIEP), a specialized institute of UNESCO, this immersive fellowship will take place at the Humanitas College of Kyung Hee University in South Korea from June 25 to July 4, 2026. This highly competitive programme brings together a select cohort of Indian youth to engage in critical discussions, experiential learning, and collaborative problem-solving around global challenges such as conflict resolution, digital transformation, and sustainable development. About UNESCO MGIEP Youth Fellowship The UNESCO MGIEP Youth Fellowship is an action-oriented programme that integrates education, leadership, and global citizenship. It is rooted in UNESCO’s broader mission of fostering peace through education and empowering young people to become changemakers in their communities. The fellowship uses real-world case studies from the Asia-Pacific region to help participants understand complex global challenges and develop innovative solutions aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Fellowship Overview Key Programme Details Fellowship Dates: June 25 – July 4, 2026 Location: Humanitas College, Kyung Hee University, South Korea Mode: Fully in-person immersive programme Application Deadline: April 25, 2026 (11:59 PM IST) Participants will be required to attend all sessions physically, making it a deeply engaging and collaborative experience. Objectives of the Fellowship The programme is designed with clear and impactful objectives that align with global development priorities. Core Objectives Develop Leadership for Peace and Sustainability Equip youth with the skills to address global and local challenges Promote values of peace, inclusion, and sustainability Build Future Changemakers Strengthen leadership capacity among Indian youth Prepare participants for impactful careers Create a Strong Global Network Connect fellows with like-minded peers Foster long-term collaboration and knowledge exchange What Participants Will Learn The fellowship curriculum is designed to provide both theoretical knowledge and practical skills. Key Learning Areas Peacebuilding and conflict transformation Digital technology and social innovation Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Leadership and critical thinking Global citizenship and ethical decision-making Participants will engage in: Interactive workshops Case study analysis Group discussions Reflective learning sessions Fellowship Benefits The UNESCO MGIEP Youth Fellowship is fully funded, ensuring that selected participants can focus entirely on learning and collaboration. Financial and Logistical Benefits International travel costs covered Visa expenses covered Accommodation provided Meals during the fellowship covered This comprehensive support removes financial barriers and ensures equal access for all selected participants. Eligibility Criteria The fellowship is specifically targeted at young Indian leaders who demonstrate potential and commitment to social impact. Who Can Apply Applicants must: Be under 30 years of age at the start of the fellowship Be a citizen of India and currently residing in India Have completed an undergraduate degree before the programme begins Be proficient in English Be available to attend the fellowship in person in South Korea Inclusivity Commitment The programme strongly encourages applications from: All genders Diverse socio-economic and cultural backgrounds Persons with disabilities This reflects UNESCO’s commitment to equity, diversity, and inclusion. Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on: Clarity of thought and vision Ability to connect past experiences with future aspirations Alignment with the fellowship’s objectives Demonstrated interest in peace and sustainability Application Process Applicants must follow the Official Application procedure. Important Instructions Applications must be submitted through the Official Online Form Applications sent via email will not be accepted All required information must be completed accurately Applicants are encouraged to carefully prepare their responses to demonstrate their motivation and potential impact. Why You Should Apply This fellowship offers a transformative opportunity for young leaders to: Gain international exposure in South Korea Learn from global experts and practitioners Develop leadership and problem-solving skills Build a global network of changemakers Contribute to peacebuilding and sustainable development Learn more about the UNESCO MGIEP Youth Fellowship on Leadership for Peace and Sustainability 2026 HERE. Discover more global Fellowship and Leadership opportunities on OFY. title: Communications Fellowship 2026 with ICTJ: A Unique Opportunity for African Professionals in Human Rights and Transitional Justice
summary: The Communications Fellowship 2026 offered under the Initiative for Transitional Justice in Africa (ITJA) presents a powerful opportunity for young African professionals to gain hands-on experience in communications within the fields of human rights, governance, and transitional justice . This fellowship is designed to amplify African expertise and strengthen communication strategies that support justice, accountability, and institutional reform across the continent. If you are passionate about storytelling, advocacy, and impactful communication, this fellowship offers a meaningful platform to contribute to real change. About the Initiative for Transitional Justice in Africa (ITJA) The Initiative for Transitional Justice in Africa (ITJA) is a three-year collaborative project launched by the African Union (AU) and the European Union (EU) . Its main objective is to support AU member states in integrating and implementing the African Union Transitional Justice Policy (AUTJP) at the national level. The project is implemented by a consortium of leading organizations: The International Center for Transitional Justice (ICTJ) (lead organization) The African Transitional Justice Legacy Fund (ATJLF) The Center for the Study of Violence and Reconciliation (CSVR) Together, these institutions work to promote justice, accountability, and sustainable peace across Africa. About the International Center for Transitional Justice (ICTJ) ICTJ is an internationally recognized nonprofit organization specializing in transitional justice. It operates globally to address the root causes and consequences of large-scale human rights violations. ICTJ’s work focuses on: Promoting accountability and justice Supporting victims and affirming their dignity Strengthening democratic institutions Preventing recurrence of violence and repression Through this fellowship, ICTJ aims to enhance communication efforts that highlight these critical issues and engage diverse audiences. Overview of the Communications Fellowship The Communications Fellowship is designed to support ITJA’s visibility and outreach efforts while building the fellow’s professional capacity in strategic communications. The selected fellow will work closely with: ITJA leadership ICTJ Communications Team Programme teams across the consortium The role focuses on creating, managing, and disseminating content that reflects the project’s impact and engages stakeholders across Africa and globally. Fellowship Duration and Structure Duration: 6 months Start Date: Immediate upon selection Mode: Flexible (virtual or in-person elements depending on activities) The fellowship offers a structured yet dynamic work environment, allowing the fellow to contribute meaningfully to ongoing projects. Key Responsibilities The Communications Fellow will play a central role in shaping how ITJA communicates its work. Responsibilities include: Content Creation and Social Media Management Draft content for platforms such as LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, Bluesky, and X Ensure alignment with donor communication guidelines Event Communications Support Prepare invitations and promotional materials Provide live coverage during events (posts, stories, photography) Draft press releases and post-event summaries Performance Monitoring Track and analyze social media engagement metrics Content Adaptation Transform long-form publications (reports, policy briefs) into accessible formats Visual Content Development Create infographics and visual materials showcasing project outcomes Editorial Support Proofread and format publications Draft website articles and program highlights Additional Support Assist the communications team with various tasks as needed Financial Support The selected fellow will receive a monthly stipend for the entire six-month duration. This ensures financial support while gaining valuable professional experience. Eligibility Criteria and Requirements Applicants must meet the following requirements: Educational Background Bachelor’s degree in: Communications Journalism International Relations Or a related field A Master’s degree is also accepted Required Skills and Experience Strong writing and editing skills Experience in communications within development, governance, or human rights sectors Familiarity with design tools such as: Canva Adobe Creative Suite Additional Requirements Demonstrated interest in transitional justice and human rights Proficiency in English (mandatory) Additional languages (French, Portuguese, or Arabic) are an advantage Application Process To apply for the Communications Fellowship, candidates must: Complete the official application form Submit the following documents: Curriculum Vitae (CV) Cover Letter (maximum one page) One or two writing samples Selection Process Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted Interviews may be conducted virtually or in person Application Deadline There is no fixed deadline for this fellowship. Applications will be reviewed continuously until a suitable candidate is selected. Early application is strongly recommended. Why You Should Apply This fellowship offers a unique opportunity to: Work at the intersection of communications and human rights Contribute to high-impact projects across Africa Build a strong professional portfolio in strategic communications Gain experience with leading international organizations Expand your network within the development and governance sectors For individuals passionate about storytelling, advocacy, and justice, this fellowship provides a meaningful and career-defining experience. title: Apply Now: Black Philanthropy Circle Grant 2026 (Nonprofit Funding Opportunity in the U.S.)
summary: The Black Philanthropy Circle Grant 2026 offers an excellent funding opportunity for nonprofit organizations dedicated to uplifting Black communities in the Baltimore metropolitan area. Managed through a donor-advised fund, this initiative supports impactful programs that improve social, economic, and cultural outcomes for African-Americans. About the Black Philanthropy Circle Fund The Black Philanthropy Giving Circle Fund is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) donor-advised fund focused on advancing charitable efforts that benefit Black communities. The fund prioritizes organizations that are either Black-led or deeply rooted in serving African-American populations within Baltimore. Grant Value Funding Amount: Up to $25,000 per grant Application Timeline Application Opens: April 1, 2026 (8:00 AM EST) Application Deadline: April 30, 2026 (4:59 PM EST) Decision Announcement: By June 30, 2026 Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit early to avoid last-minute technical issues or incomplete submissions. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the Black Philanthropy Circle Grant, organizations must: Be a 501(c)(3) nonprofit (or have a fiscal sponsor with this status) Be Black-led or based in Black communities Operate within the Baltimore metropolitan area Provide programs or services that directly benefit Black individuals and communities Priority Funding Areas While applications across various sectors are considered, priority is given to organizations working in: Arts Culture Economic Welfare (e.g., job placement, workforce training) Education, Recreation, and Youth Development Community Organizing and Service (non-political) Environmental Initiatives Human Services Health and Wellness Application Guidelines Ensure all required documents and information are prepared before starting your application Submit a complete application —incomplete submissions will not be reviewed Applications submitted after the deadline will not be accepted How to Apply Once the application portal opens on April 1, 2026, eligible organizations can begin their application through the official portal by clicking the “ Apply Now” . The Black Philanthropy Circle Grant represents a meaningful opportunity for organizations committed to driving positive change in Black communities. Early preparation and submission are key to ensuring your application is considered. If your organization aligns with the mission and eligibility criteria, don’t miss this chance to secure funding and expand your impact. Discover more Grant opportunity here. title: Internship in Social Sciences at ISEC Bengaluru 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Postgraduate Students
summary: The Institute for Social and Economic Change (ISEC) in Bengaluru, India, has officially opened applications for its 2026 Social Sciences Internship Programme , offering postgraduate students a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in interdisciplinary research. This prestigious internship is designed for students who are eager to deepen their understanding of social science research, engage with leading academics, and contribute to policy-relevant studies. With a strong legacy of research excellence and policy impact, ISEC provides an ideal environment for young researchers to develop critical skills and practical knowledge. About the Institute for Social and Economic Change (ISEC) Established in 1972, ISEC is one of India’s leading interdisciplinary research institutions dedicated to advancing knowledge in social sciences. The institute has played a significant role in shaping public policy through rigorous research in areas such as economics, sociology, political science, and education. ISEC is widely recognized for: Conducting high-quality macro and micro-level research Promoting equity and social justice Bridging the gap between research and policy-making Hosting a distinguished team of economists, sociologists, political scientists, and educationists A major focus of the institute is capacity building among young researchers , making this internship a valuable stepping stone for students aiming to pursue academic or policy-oriented careers. Objectives of the ISEC Internship Programme The primary goal of the programme is to provide postgraduate students with practical exposure to interdisciplinary research in social sciences. This is achieved through: 1. Hands-on Research Experience Interns will actively participate in research projects, gaining insight into methodologies, data analysis, and academic writing. 2. Developing Research Skills Students will be guided in: Formulating research questions Designing research frameworks Conducting independent or collaborative studies 3. Academic Interaction Interns will engage with: Faculty members Researchers PhD scholars from nine academic centres within ISEC. 4. Understanding Policy Dynamics Participants will gain first-hand exposure to how research informs policy decisions, including situations where research and policy may align or diverge. Internship Duration and Format The 2026 internship will be conducted entirely in offline mode , requiring physical presence in Bengaluru. Duration: 2 months Period: Between May and August 2026 Commitment: Full-time ISEC offers flexibility in scheduling within this timeframe, but interns are expected to maintain consistent engagement and collaborate closely with faculty supervisors. Stipend, Accommodation, and Travel Benefits The internship provides financial and logistical support to ensure accessibility for selected candidates. Stipend INR 5,000 per month (for two months) Awarded based on performance, diligence, and deliverables Accommodation Free accommodation (excluding food) Provided at ISEC hostels, guest houses, or quarters Available only for students coming from outside Bengaluru Travel Support Reimbursement of train travel (non-AC sleeper class) Applicable for interns traveling from outside Bengaluru Eligibility Criteria To apply for this internship, candidates must: Be currently pursuing a postgraduate degree in social sciences Be between the 2nd and 3rd semesters of their Master’s programme This ensures that applicants have foundational academic knowledge while still benefiting from early research exposure. Research Themes Available ISEC offers a wide range of interdisciplinary research areas, allowing students to align their interests with ongoing projects. 1. Agriculture Includes agricultural economics, rural development, agribusiness, econometrics, and AI/ML applications in agriculture. 2. Environment and Climate Change Focuses on socioecology, environmental valuation, and climate change analysis. 3. Economic Studies Covers development economics, trade, industrial economics, public finance, and socio-economic impact analysis. 4. Urban and Gender Studies Explores urban governance, poverty, informality, land use, transport, and gender-related issues. 5. Education and Employment Includes labour markets, migration, education systems, and human capital development. 6. Public Health and Population Addresses public health systems, population studies, and medical anthropology. 7. Decentralisation and Development Focuses on local governance, cooperatives, and fiscal decentralisation. Application Process Applicants must follow a structured application procedure: Download the application form from the official ISEC website Complete the form carefully Attach two recommendation letters : One from the Head of Department One from a senior faculty member Send the complete application, Application Deadline 21 April 2026 Applicants are strongly advised to submit their applications early, as ISEC will not be responsible for postal delays. All submissions will undergo a rigorous selection process, and decisions made by the selection committee will be final. Why You Should Apply This internship is a rare opportunity to: Work with leading experts in social sciences Gain practical research experience Understand the relationship between research and policy Build a strong academic and professional network Strengthen your profile for future careers in academia, public policy, or international organizations For postgraduate students seeking meaningful exposure and career growth, the ISEC Internship 2026 stands out as a highly valuable and enriching experience. For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post Internship in Social Sciences at ISEC Bengaluru 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Postgraduate Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNICEF Climate and Health Innovation Challenge 2026: Secure Up to $100,000 Equity-Free Funding for Startups Transforming Children’s Health Worldwide
summary: The UNICEF Climate Health Innovation Challenge 2026, powered by the UNICEF Office of Innovation, is a high-impact global funding opportunity designed for startups building cutting-edge solutions at the intersection of climate change and public health. This initiative is part of the broader UNICEF Venture Fund, which invests in transformative technologies that improve the lives of children across the globe. Overview of the UNICEF Climate Health Innovation Challenge The UNICEF Climate Innovation Challenge seeks to identify, support, and scale innovative solutions that protect children from the growing threats of climate change. These threats include extreme heat, floods, drought, and air pollution—all of which directly impact children s health, safety, and access to essential services. Through this initiative, UNICEF aims to: Discover high-impact, scalable innovations Strengthen climate resilience systems for children Support early-stage startups in emerging markets Provide technical mentorship and investment readiness support The program specifically focuses on solutions that address climate-related health risks affecting children, making it one of the most targeted and impactful funding opportunities in the global innovation space. Funding Benefits and What Selected Startups Receive Successful applicants gain access to a comprehensive support system, including: 1. Equity-Free Funding Up to $ 100,000 in funding for early-stage startups 2. Mentorship and Technical Support At least 10 hours of expert mentoring Guidance in: Open-source development Business strategy Software engineering Diversity, equity, and inclusion 3. Access to UNICEF’s Global Network Opportunities to collaborate with: Governments NGOs Corporate partners Pilot programs in UNICEF country offices 4. Investment Readiness Support Assistance in preparing for future funding rounds Exposure to global investors and partners Focus Areas: Climate and Health Solutions The challenge emphasizes innovations that protect children from climate-induced health risks. Examples include: Air pollution monitoring and mitigation tools Climate-resilient healthcare systems Early warning systems for climate disasters Digital health solutions for vulnerable communities Water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) innovations These solutions must demonstrate real-world applicability and scalability, particularly in low-resource environments. Eligibility Criteria: Who Can Apply Category of Applicants This opportunity is open to: Early-stage and growth-stage startups For-profit companies Technology-driven innovators Required Qualifications Applicants must meet the following mandatory criteria: Be a registered private company Be based in a UNICEF programme country Have a working prototype or minimum viable product (MVP) Be open-source or willing to adopt open-source licensing Demonstrate potential to impact vulnerable children positively Provide measurable, real-time data outputs Eligible Countries and Regions The opportunity is primarily targeted at emerging markets, meaning applicants can come from: Africa Asia Latin America Middle East Other UNICEF programme countries worldwide This global inclusivity ensures that innovators from underserved regions—where climate impacts are often most severe—can access funding and scale their solutions. Ideal Candidates: Who Should Apply This opportunity is best suited for: Tech startups working on climate-health intersections Founders building solutions for low-resource settings Innovators using technologies like: Artificial Intelligence Blockchain Data science Teams focused on social impact and child welfare UNICEF also encourages applications from: Women-led startups Youth innovators Underrepresented communities in tech Key Features That Make a Strong Application To stand out, your solution should: Address real challenges affecting children Be scalable across multiple regions Function effectively in low-connectivity environments Promote equity and inclusion Support multiple languages, including local languages Why This Opportunity Matters Climate change is one of the greatest threats to children globally. From disrupted education to increased disease exposure, the impact is profound and long-lasting. UNICEF’s innovation approach ensures that solutions are: Sustainable Scalable Child-centered By participating in this challenge, innovators are not only accessing funding—they are contributing to a global mission to protect future generations. How to Apply Interested applicants should visit the official UNICEF Innovation page and submit their applications before the deadline. Ensure that all required documentation, including product details and impact metrics, is prepared in advance. Apply here Deadline 17th May 2026 The UNICEF Climate Health Innovation Challenge 2026 represents a rare opportunity to combine technology, impact, and global scale. For startups committed to solving some of the world’s most pressing challenges, this is more than funding—it is a platform for global transformation. If you are building a solution that can safeguard children’s health in the face of climate change, this is your chance to gain international recognition, funding, and support from one of the most respected organizations in the world.
